title: Testing, Red Teaming, Release, Rollback, and Production Defense · Guided Program Studio
summary: A retrieval engineer and domain partner incrementally build production RAG release and defense dossier, change one query, document, tenant, language, model, index, or policy condition, and keep earlier Python, provenance, accessibility, security, evaluation, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of production RAG release and defense dossier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Build deterministic unit, property, parser, chunk, retrieval, reranking, context, citation, contract, integration, end-to-end, and changed-corpus tests with fixed seeds and versions.
Red team poisoning, indirect injection, embedding manipulation, tenant leaks, secret extraction, unsafe output, tool effects, denial of service, deletion, and incident response.
Verify keyboard, landmarks, headings, focus, status, errors, citations, tables, text alternatives, touch, reduced motion, responsive layouts, comprehension, and escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Testing Pyramid Fixtures
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop RAG engineering team handles case 3094 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate release on component, end-to-end, security, accessibility, load, rollback, and recovery evidence; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Build deterministic unit, property, parser, chunk, retrieval, reranking, context, citation, contract, integration, end-to-end, and changed-corpus tests with fixed seeds and versions.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-production-defense-oi4jpt" that uses case 5231 to gate release on component, end-to-end, security, accessibility, load, rollback, and recovery evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Production Defense, keep Rag Production Defense connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-production-defense-oi4jpt
# Competency: rag-production-defense.
# Case 5231: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def evidence_rag_production_defense_oi4jpt(unit: bool = True, retrieval_eval: bool = True, generation_eval: bool = True, security: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, load: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_oi4jpt = "oi4jpt-tpj4io-5231"
    assert evidence_variant_oi4jpt
    evidence_axes_oi4jpt = (
        "corpusoi4jpt",
        "querytpj4io",
        "tenant5231",
        "indexoi4jpt5231",
        "prompttpj4io5231",
        "evaluation5231oi4jpt",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_oi4jpt)) == 6
    gates = (unit, retrieval_eval, generation_eval, security, accessibility, load, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not unit or not retrieval_eval:
        return False, passed, "deterministic-gates"
    if not generation_eval or not security or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-safety-gates"
    if not load or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "production-gates"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"
